about: PitCast reproducible open benchmark v0 — built only on cited in-repo data.

cpt_leave_one_out.basis: ASTM G48-type FeCl3 immersion (headline); all-method LOO shown for transparency

cases.case: 304 stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
cases.source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161

cases.case: 316L stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
cases.source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161

cases.case: 317L stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
cases.source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161

cases.case: 904L (N08904) - CPT in 1 M NaCl
cases.source: Sun Y.T., Wang J.M., Jiang Y.M., Li J., Materials and Corrosion 69 (2018) 44

cases.case: 254 SMO (S31254, 6Mo) - CPT in 1 M NaCl
cases.source: Sun Y.T., Wang J.M., Jiang Y.M., Li J., Materials and Corrosion 69 (2018) 44

cases.case: 2205 (S31803/S32205) duplex - CPT in acidified ferric chloride
cases.source: Hoseinpoor M., Momeni M., Moayed M.H., Davoodi A., Corrosion Sci. 80 (2014) 197

cases.case: 254 SMO (S31254, 6Mo) - CPT by ASTM G48 Method E
cases.source: Outokumpu, 'Testing stainless steel for resistance to pitting corrosion' (2023)

cases.case: Nesic 2007 - 5% CO2 flow-loop at 60 C
cases.source: Nesic S., Corrosion Sci. 49 (2007) 4308; Ohio U flow-loop

cases.case: Tengiz (KZ) sour-sweet field - Kahyarian 2017
cases.note: sour-sweet; FeS co-film usually lowers the sweet-model rate
cases.source: Kahyarian A., Singer M., Nesic S., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 41 (2017) 14

cases.case: Top-of-line corrosion - Singer 2017
cases.note: top-of-line condensing water; bulk flowline models are not TLC models
cases.source: Singer M., Camacho A., Brown B., Nesic S., Corrosion 73 (2017) 1030

cases.case: 13Cr OCTG threshold - Mishina 2014
cases.note: 13Cr CRA passivates above ~120 C; carbon-steel CO2 models do not apply
cases.source: Mishina K. et al., NACE Paper 4226 (2014)

cases.case: HP/HT well @ 175 C, 100 bar CO2 - Nyborg 2007
cases.note: protective FeCO3 scale dominates at high T
cases.source: Nyborg R., NACE Paper 07522 (2007)

cases.case: Elgaddafi 2015 - autoclave 80 C, 4.1 bar CO2
cases.note: stagnant 2-L autoclave (8.9 cm dia); 187 h steady-state LPR; scale-forming at 80 C; pH omitted -> computed in-situ (Crolet-Bonis), no added bicarbonate
cases.source: Elgaddafi R. et al., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 27 (2015) 1620

cases.case: Elgaddafi 2015 - autoclave 80 C, 6.2 bar CO2
cases.note: stagnant 2-L autoclave; 187 h steady-state LPR; scale-forming at 80 C; pH computed in-situ
cases.source: Elgaddafi R. et al., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 27 (2015) 1620

cases.case: Peng 2024 - L360N blank, CO2-saturated 5% NaCl, 40 C
cases.note: CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 1 - P_H2O(40C) ~ 0.93 bar); 5 wt% NaCl; 72 h weight-loss; quiescent cell; pH omitted -> computed in-situ
cases.source: Peng L. et al., Materials 17(5) (2024) 1094

cases.case: Aristia 2019 - St-37 geothermal brine, 70 C, CO2 purged
cases.note: CO2 purged at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 1 - P_H2O(70C) ~ 0.69 bar); low-Cl geothermal brine; 7 d; pH 4 measured
cases.source: Aristia G., Hoa L.Q., Bassler R., Materials 12(22) (2019) 3801

cases.case: Tang 2018 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 3.5% NaCl, 60 C
cases.note: CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 0.8 bar at 60 C); 3.5% NaCl; 72 h; measured converted from 0.345 g/m2/h -> 0.385 mm/y; pH computed in-situ
cases.source: Tang J. et al., Molecules 23(12) (2018) 3270

cases.case: Imidazoline RSC 2019 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 2% NaCl, 20 C
cases.note: CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 1 - P_H2O(20C) ~ 0.99 bar); 2 wt% NaCl; pH 4.11 measured; 72 h weight-loss; quiescent 500 mL cell; Q235
cases.source: Corrosion inhibition of mild steel by an imidazoline hydrolysate in CO2-saturated solution, RSC Adv. 9 (2019), Table 1

cases.case: Imidazoline RSC 2019 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 2% NaCl, 60 C
cases.note: CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 1 - P_H2O(60C) ~ 0.81 bar); 2 wt% NaCl; pH 4.11 measured; 72 h weight-loss; quiescent cell; Q235
cases.source: Corrosion inhibition of mild steel by an imidazoline hydrolysate in CO2-saturated solution, RSC Adv. 9 (2019), Table 1

cases.case: Coatings 2025 - blank, CO2-sat chloride-carbonate brine 60 C (AISI 1018)
cases.note: Uninhibited blank, AISI 1018, 30 g/L NaCl + 0.1 g/L NaHCO3 + 0.1 g/L CaCO3, CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~ 1 - P_H2O(60C) ~ 0.8 bar), 72 h mass loss, quiescent; pH computed in-situ. Coatings 15(1):93 (2025), DOI 10.3390/coatings15010093.
cases.source: Coatings 15(1):93 (2025) - uninhibited (blank) corrosion rate v0 (oleic-acid-imidazoline/CeCl3 inhibitor study)

cases.case: Thiophene-imidazoline RSC 2025 - Q235 blank, CO2-sat formation water, 90 C
cases.note: Blank Q235 weight-loss, CO2-saturated formation water at 1 atm (pCO2~0.31 bar at 90 C), 22 h, pH 4.13. Text-stated: 'at 90 C, the corrosion rate of carbon steel in the inhibitor-free solution reaches 1.8115 mm/a'. Highest-T CO2 anchor in corpus.
cases.source: Jia et al., RSC Adv. (2025), DOI 10.1039/d5ra04201a - inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate (text, Sec. 3.2)

cases.case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 20 C
cases.note: Blank X65, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl ~1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 measured; quiescent.
cases.source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)

cases.case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 30 C
cases.note: Blank X65, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl ~1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 measured; quiescent.
cases.source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)

cases.case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 40 C
cases.note: Blank X65, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl ~1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 measured; quiescent.
cases.source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)

cases.case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 50 C
cases.note: Blank X65, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl ~1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 measured; quiescent.
cases.source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)

b31g_burst.basis: predicted failure pressure P_f (SF=1) vs measured burst; specimen actual yield used as SMYS

sources.BENJAMIN2016: Benjamin A.C., Freire J.L.F., Vieira R.D., Cunha D.J.S., Interaction of corrosion defects in pipelines - Part 2: MTI JIP database of corroded pipe tests, Int. J. Press. Vessels Pip. 145 (2016) 41-59, DOI 10.1016/j.ijpvp.2016.06.006. PRIMARY source of the IDTS 13-30 series: full-scale hydrotest-to-failure of API 5L X70 (OD 457.2 mm, t ~7.9 mm) with spark-eroded defect colonies. Measured failure pressures = Table 8; cluster geometry = Table 6; per-tube yields = Table 1.
sources.EFA2020: Mousavi S.S., Moghaddam A.S., Failure pressure estimation error for corroded pipeline using various revisions of ASME B31G, Engineering Failure Analysis 108 (2020) 104284, DOI 10.1016/j.engfailanal.2019.104284. Secondary tabulation of the same IDTS series (its Table 4); used to cross-verify every value.
sources.QI2023: Qi C. et al., Experiments and evaluation on residual strength of X52 steel pipe with various internal defects, Front. Energy Res. 10 (2023) 1046900, DOI 10.3389/fenrg.2022.1046900
sources.CRONIN2000: Cronin D.S., "Assessment of Corrosion Defects in Pipelines", PhD thesis, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, University of Waterloo, 2000 (UWSpace, open access: uwspace.uwaterloo.ca). Full-scale burst tests of pipe sections removed from operating pipelines, each containing a single isolated REAL corrosion defect. Geometry (OD, wall thickness, SMYS/actual YS/UTS), measured failure pressure, max defect depth and defect length from Table 4.2.1; per-model predictions (original B31G, RSTRENG, PCORRC, Ritchie&Last) from the page-182 table. Tables extracted with PyMuPDF find_tables(); every stored specimen's geometry was independently VERIFIED by reproducing Cronin's own tabulated original-B31G prediction with b31g.js to within a systematic -2..-6% (a B31G-variant offset that is the same sign across ALL specimens, confirming correct column extraction). 8 of 40 rows were excluded because their defect depth/length did not extract cleanly.
sources.HEZHAO2024: He P., Zhao B., "Experimental and Numerical Analysis of the Impact of Corrosion on the Failure Pressure of API 5L X65 Pipeline", J. Mar. Sci. Eng. 12(10):1810, 2024, DOI 10.3390/jmse12101810 (MDPI, open access). Test Pipeline 1 hydrostatic burst: OD 325 mm, t 12 mm (Table 3), defect length 395 mm (l/D 1.215) and max depth 9.3 mm (d/t 0.775) (Table 5), measured failure pressure 11.4 MPa (Sec. 3.4, text). API 5L X65, actual yield 545 MPa (Table 2). Tests 2-4 reported only as normalized pf/p0 and were excluded (no directly-tabulated absolute burst pressure).
sources.SOUZA2007_P4: Souza R.D., Benjamin A.C., Vieira R.D., Freire J.L.F., Castro J.T.P., "Part 4: Rupture tests of pipeline segments containing long real corrosion defects", Experimental Techniques 31(1) (2007) 46-51, DOI 10.1111/j.1747-1567.2006.00134.x (Wiley; accessed via METU). Full-scale burst tests, single isolated irregular-shaped REAL internal corrosion defects (API 5L X42 OD273, X46 OD457). Geometry Table 4, measured transverse-tensile yield Table 2, measured burst + B31G prediction Table 5. b31g.js reproduces the paper's own original-B31G prediction EXACTLY (0.0% diff) for all 7, confirming extraction.
sources.FREIRE2006_P3: Benjamin A.C., Freire J.L.F., Vieira R.D. et al., pipeline-integrity "Part 3" burst-test series, Experimental Techniques (2006), DOI 10.1111/j.1747-1567.2006.00109.x (Wiley; accessed via METU). Full-scale burst tests, single machined/spark-eroded longitudinal metal-loss defects (API 5L X60 OD324 & OD508, X80 OD457/459 short, X46 & A25 OD76.2). Geometry Table 3 (max defect depth d = wall t minus tabulated minimum defect thickness t*), measured yield means Table 3, measured burst + B31G Table 3. b31g.js reproduces the paper's own original-B31G EXACTLY (<=2.3% diff) for all 16, confirming extraction + the d=t-t* depth.
